articles.title: Reg Austin obituary
articles.description: Courageous lawyer who helped Zimbabwe gain independence and oversaw elections in Cambodia and Afghanistan

articles.title: Farmers unite to tackle climate challenges, boost yields
articles.description: Obey Musiwa-Herald Reporter As climate pressures intensify across Zimbabwe, farmers and Government stakeholders gathered at Mudoti Farm in Chikomba West Constituency for the provincial field day, aiming to strengthen productivity...

articles.title: Protecting lions and people: the biologist dedicated to tackling human-wildlife conflict
articles.description: Moreangels Mbizah has blazed a trail in Zimbabwe as the first black African woman to found a conservation organisation in the country

more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: “You Will Be Arrested”: ZRP Warns Drivers Against Overloading
more.description: The Zimbabwe Republic Police, ZRP, has warned public service vehicle operators and transporters against overloading and carrying passengers, luggage and goods beyond their stipulated capacity.

more.title: Cooking Gas Price Hiked For Second Month Running
more.description: The Zimbabwe Energy Regulatory Authority (ZERA) has raised the price of Liquid Petroleum Gas (LPG) for the second time in as many months.

more.title: New IPEC powers to deepen Zim’s global financial integration
more.description: Source: New IPEC powers to deepen Zim’s global financial integration – herald Business Reporter LEGAL REFORMS to Zimbabwe’s insurance and pensions sector are set to deepen the country’s global financial integration by granting the Insurance and Pensions Commission (IPEC) enhanced powers for cross-border regulatory cooperation. more.title: Consumer Protection Policy to boost quality, fairness

more.title: Diamond giant hits critical ‘breaking point’

more.title: Leather sector SMEs call for access to affordable finance
more.description: SMALL to medium enterprises in the leather sector have called for measures to improve access to affordable capital needed to upgrade machinery and enhance product quality to compete with imports. Zimbabwe’s leather sector is undergoing a structural evolution, transitioning from a reliance on raw hide exports to a high-value, design-driven production model anchored by the [...] The post Leather sector SMEs call for access to affordable finance appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail .

more.title: Chiyangwa challenges graduates to create jobs, build economy
more.description: HARARE – Prominent businessman Dr Philip Chiyangwa has urged empowerment graduates to transition from job seekers to employment creators, leveraging their practical skills to stimulate Zimbabwe’s grassroots economy. Speaking as the guest of honour at The Tonganyika Trust graduation in Chinhoyi last Sunday — where he presided over the certification of 273 graduates — Dr [...] The post Chiyangwa challenges graduates to create jobs, build economy appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail .

more.title: China says April exports jump 14.1% from a year ago ahead of Trump-Xi summit
more.description: HONG KONG — China’s exports rose 14.1% in April from a year earlier, the government said Saturday, despite the Iran war and lingering impacts from higher U.S. tariffs. The data were released just days ahead of a planned meeting next week between U.S. President Donald Trump and Chinese leader Xi Jinping in Beijing. That beat [...] The post China says April exports jump 14.1% from a year ago ahead of Trump-Xi summit appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail .

more.title: Digital Twins and the Future of Infrastructure Investment in Zimbabwe
more.description: Why Zimbabwe Can No Longer Afford Inefficient Infrastructure Planning Zimbabwe is entering a period where infrastructure investment decisions will increasingly define the country’s long-term economic trajectory. From roads and railways to energy systems, water infrastructure, and urban transport, the pressure to modernise public assets is growing rapidly. Yet the country faces a fundamental challenge: finite [...] The post Digital Twins and the Future of Infrastructure Investment in Zimbabwe appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail .

more.title: Engineering, Construction and Building Materials in Zimbabwe: Building Value in a Transforming Economic Landscape
more.description: A Sector Under Structural Recalibration Zimbabwe’s engineering, construction, and building materials sector is undergoing a profound structural transition shaped by shifting macroeconomic conditions, constrained public investment, rapid urbanisation, and increasing demand for resilient infrastructure. From Harare’s expanding residential estates to major transport corridor upgrades such as the Beitbridge–Harare highway and ongoing industrial redevelopment in Bulawayo, [...] The post Engineering, Construction and Building Materials in Zimbabwe: Building Value in a Transforming Economic Landscape appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail .
